note: Probes the Authorization provider plugin, its lack of its own admin route, mapping/regex config and revocation/sync-on-logon behaviour. Recipe (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, how does ldap_authorization integrate with the Authorization module, and what is the provider plugin id and class?
evals.expected_output: It registers an @AuthorizationProvider plugin id 'ldap_provider' (class LDAPAuthorizationProvider) that the Authorization module uses to grant consumers such as Drupal roles from LDAP groups.

evals.prompt: Where do I configure LDAP-group-to-Drupal-role mapping — does ldap_authorization have its own settings page?
evals.expected_output: ldap_authorization has no admin route of its own; you configure it inside an Authorization module profile, pairing the ldap_provider provider with a consumer (e.g. Drupal roles).

evals.prompt: In an ldap_authorization mapping, which two config keys hold the LDAP group to match and the flag that treats it as a regular expression?
evals.expected_output: The 'query' key (the LDAP group DN/value) and the 'is_regex' boolean, in authorization.provider_mappings.plugin.ldap_provider.

evals.prompt: Does the LDAP authorization provider support removing Drupal roles when a user leaves an LDAP group, and re-checking on login?
evals.expected_output: Yes; the plugin declares revocationSupported = TRUE (roles revoked when the group is lost) and syncOnLogonSupported = TRUE (re-evaluated on each login).

evals.prompt: Which provider setting selects the LDAP server used to read group membership, and which one restricts mapping to LDAP-authenticated users only?
evals.expected_output: status.server picks the ldap_server to read groups from; status.only_ldap_authenticated restricts the mapping to users authenticated via LDAP.

evals.prompt: Which modules must be enabled for ldap_authorization to work on Drupal 11?
evals.expected_output: It depends on ldap_servers, ldap_user and the authorization module (plus, transitively, symfony/ldap and externalauth).
